# Workflows — Vars

Var definitions are derived from `module.lowdefy.yaml`. Pass these via the `vars:` block in your app's `lowdefy.yaml` entry for this module.

## Summary

| Name | Type | Default | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `workflows_config` | array |  | Yes | Array of workflow definitions consumed by every build-time resolver in this module (parts 4, 12, 13, 15). Host app supplies via the entry's `vars` block, e.g. `vars: { _ref: workflow_config/workflows.yaml }`. Each entry is a workflow object with `type`, a required `entity:` block, `starting_actions`, `action_groups`, and `actions` (see workflows-module-concept/action-authoring/spec.md). The `entity:` block carries the workflow's entity routing: - `connection_id` (required) — the entity's Lowdefy connection id (e.g. `leads-collection`). - `ref_key` (required) — the event-references key for the workflow's entity (e.g. `lead_ids`),   written into event docs so events surface on the entity. - `page_id` (required) — host-app page id rendering the entity (the entity   breadcrumb crumb links here). - `id_query_key` (optional, default `_id`) — URL query-string key for the entity's primary id. - `title` (required) — singular human-readable entity-kind label (e.g. "Lead"). - `data` (optional) — an inline routine, authored exactly like a hook   (`{ routine: [ ... ] }`), that returns an object describing the entity   instance. The module generates an engine-only `{type}-entity-data`   endpoint from it; the action read handlers call it server-side (payload   `{ entity_id }`) and surface the result on the response. Its reserved   `name` key is lifted onto the breadcrumb / back-link as the instance   display name; all other keys are host-owned and available on the action   response's `entity` object and via the `entity_view` slot. Omit it and   chrome falls back to the `title` type label and no endpoint is emitted. - `list_page_id` + `list_title` (optional pair) — the host-app entity-LIST   page id and its (plural) crumb label. Set both to prepend a list crumb to   the action-page breadcrumb so the trail mirrors the host app's own entity   pages: `Home / {list_title} / {entity} / {workflow} / {action}`. Omit both   to drop the list crumb. Setting only one is an authoring error (a link   needs both a target and a label). Each workflow also accepts an optional `entity_view:` block — a build-time, read-only UI hint, NOT part of the materialized engine config (it never reaches the validated engine output). It carries a single `slot` (a Lowdefy block ref) rendering a read-only view of the entity. On form action pages the slot is the RHS Details tab; on the check page it is the middle review subject. Omit it and the Details tab is dropped (form pages) / the middle review subject is empty (check page). Workflows, actions, and action groups accept an optional `title:`; when omitted it is derived from the slug (`type`/`id`) by the title humanizer — splits on `-`/`_`/camelCase, applies Title Case, lowercases minor words mid-string, uppercases acronyms (base set: PO ID URL API CRM SLA KPI VAT PDF CSV FAQ KYC RFQ; extended by `title_acronyms`). Set `title:` only when the default is wrong.  |
| `app_name` | string |  | Yes | The host app's deployment name. Serves three roles: (1) filters action access via `access.{app_name}` per action; (2) keys the engine's default log event display block (matching the events module's display_key projection); and (3) keys the per-app action display the engine writes onto each action doc — display surfaces read `action.{app_name}.message` and `action.{app_name}.links` for the current app's slug.  |
| `enable_internal_comments` | boolean | `false` |  | Opt this app in to the shared/internal comment distinction (Part 61). The host wires it onto the workflow-api connection (`enable_internal_comments`) and the comment surfaces read it at build time. When `true`, every comment input shows a small shared/internal control and the engine honours an `internal` (single-app) comment from this app. When `false` (the default), the control is hidden, and the engine coerces any `internal` request to `shared` — so every comment is visible to all apps that see the event. Comments default to `shared` regardless. Set this only on the internal/team app that sometimes needs to keep a note off other apps; single-app and customer-facing apps leave it off.  |
| `action_statuses_display` | object | `{}` |  | Per-status display overrides for the shipped action_statuses enum. Merged via `_build.object.assign` onto the shipped enum and exposed as the `action_statuses` component for UI consumption only. The engine reads the shipped enum directly from the module file — overrides are UI-only and cannot affect engine behaviour (transition legality is owned by the per-kind FSM tables; the enum's `priority` is display-only ordering).  |
| `workflow_lifecycle_stages_display` | object | `{}` |  | Per-stage display overrides for the shipped workflow_lifecycle_stages enum. Same merge semantics as action_statuses_display: UI-only, cannot affect engine logic.  |
| `title_acronyms` | array | `[]` |  | Domain acronyms (e.g. [BOM, SKU]) merged into the module's base acronym set and applied by the title humanizer when deriving default titles from workflow/action/group slugs. A token whose lowercased form matches an entry is fully uppercased in derived titles (e.g. "upload-bom" → "Upload BOM"). Base set ships in the module; this var extends it. Has no effect on explicitly authored `title:` values.  |
| `contacts_collection` | string | `user-contacts` |  | Collection name the workflow-api connection's contact lookups join to resolve each contact's avatar (the action-page read GetWorkflowAction joins it to project profile.picture). Defaults to `user-contacts` — the shared collection where a user IS a contact (same _id space). Set this to match your contacts collection name if the host app points the contacts module at a custom collection. Tenant wall: the workflow-api connection is tenant-scoped, so a substituted collection must itself carry the `organizationId` field — without it the join fails closed.  |
